Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (Xi Jinping) is one of the most powerful leaders in the world as the Prime Minister of China and the General Secretary of the Communist Party. His biography is described step by step below:

1. Birth and Childhood (1953-1968)
Birth: 18 August, 1953 in the city of Beijing of China. His father Xi Zhongxun (Xi Zhongxun) was a former leader of the Communist Party and the Prime Minister of China (1975-1981). Mother was the wife of Xi Zhongxun and a teacher.
Childhood: He was admitted to Beijing University and studied chemical technology. His childhood life was normal and he became associated with the Communist Party when his father was a leader of the Communist Party.
2. Education and Early Career (1968-1975)
During the Cultural Revolution (1968-1975): He worked in the agricultural field in Shaanxi province during the Cultural Revolution (as a common worker). This experience was important for building his relationship with the people.
Early Career: He worked in several several agricultural fields in Shaanxi province and later worked in several several agricultural fields of Shaanxi province. During this time he became deeply involved with the Communist Party.
3. Becoming Associated with the Communist Party (1975-1982)
Becoming Associated with the Communist Party: In 1975 he became a member of the Communist Party. His father Xi Zhongxun was a leader of the Communist Party, because of which his becoming associated with the Communist Party became easy.
Early Career: He worked in several agricultural fields in Shaanxi province and later worked in several agricultural fields of Shaanxi province. During this time he became deeply involved with the Communist Party.
